person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
William I de Ferrers, 3rd Earl of Derby (died 1190) was a 12th-century English Earl who resided in Tutbury Castle in Staffordshire and was head of a family which controlled a large part of Derbyshire known as Duffield Frith. He was also a Knight Templar.

William was the son of Robert de Ferrers, 2nd Earl of Derby and his wife, Margaret Peverel. He succeeded his father as Earl of Derby in 1162. He was married to Sybil, the daughter of William de Braose, 3rd Lord of Bramber and Bertha of Hereford.

William de Ferrers was one of the earls who joined the rebellion against King Henry II of England led by Henry's eldest son, Henry the Younger, in the Revolt of 1173-1174, sacking the town of Nottingham. Robert de Ferrers II, his father, had supported Stephen of England and, although Henry II had accepted him at court, he had denied the title of earl of Derby to him and his son. In addition, William had a grudge against Henry because he believed he should have inherited the lands of Peveril Castle through his mother. These, King Henry had previously confiscated in 1155 when William Peverel fell into disfavour.

With the failure of the revolt, de Ferrers was taken prisoner by King Henry, at Northampton on the 31 July 1174, along with the King of Scots and the earls of Chester and Lincoln, along with a number of his Derbyshire underlings and was held at Caen. He was deprived of his castles at Tutbury and Duffield and both were put out of commission (and possibly Pilsbury.) In addition to defray the costs of the war Henry levied a so-called "Forest Fine" of 200 marks.

He seems to have afterwards regained the confidence of Henry II., and he showed his fidelity to the next Sovereign, (King Richard I.), by accompanying him in his expedition to the Holy Land, and joined the Third Crusade and died at the Siege of Acre in 1190.

He was succeeded by his son William de Ferrers, 4th Earl of Derby.

William de Ferrars Preceptory No.530 is a Knight Templar preceptory named after William de Ferrars. This preceptory is stationed in Burton upon Trent.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby於1136年出生在英國斯塔福爾時，他的父親Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby37歲，母親Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby36歲。他與Sybil de Braose結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1189年12月31日在巴勒斯坦منطقة الشمالعكّا享年53歲，埋葬在以色列מחוז ירושלים也盧沙阿萊姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby于1136年出生在英国斯塔福爾时，他的父亲Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby37岁，母亲Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby36岁。他与Sybil de Braose结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1189年12月31日在巴勒斯坦منطقة الشمالعكّا享年53岁，埋葬在以色列מחוז ירושלים也盧沙阿萊姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby was born in 1136, in Staffordshire, England, his father, Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby, was 37 and his mother, Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby, was 36. He married Sybil de Braose. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 31 December 1189, in Acre, Northern, Palestine, at the age of 53, and was buried in Yerushalayim, Jerusalem, Israel.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby est né en 1136, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Sybil de Braose. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 31 décembre 1189, à Acre, Northern, Palestine, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Yerushalayim, Jerusalem, Israël.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby 1136 in Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby 37 und seine Mutter Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby 36. Er hat Sybil de Braose geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 31. Dezember 1189 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Acre, Northern, Palästina und wurde in Yerushalayim, Jerusalem, Israel begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby è nato il nel 1136 nel Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Sybil de Braose. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 31 dicembre 1189, nel Acre, Northern, Palestina, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Yerushalayim, Jerusalem, Israele.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derbyが1136年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derbyは37歳，母Margaret Peverell Countess of Derbyは36歳であった。Sybil de Braoseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1189年12月31日にパレスチナمنطقة الشمالعكّاで53歳で死去し，イスラエルמחוז ירושליםイエルシャレイイムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby(이)가 1136년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Sybil de Braose(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1189년 12월 31일 팔레스타인 منطقة الشمال عكّا에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 이스라엘 מחוז ירושלים 예루샤레이임에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby nasceu em 1136, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Sybil de Braose. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 31 de dezembro de 1189, em Acre, Northern, Palestina, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Yerushalayim, Jerusalem, Israel.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby родился в 1136 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Sybil de Braose. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 31 декабря 1189 г. в местности عكّا, منطقة الشمال, Палестина в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности ииршэлеиим, מחוז ירושלים, Израиль.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby nació en 1136, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Ferrers 2nd Earl of Derby, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Margaret Peverell Countess of Derby, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Sybil de Braose. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió el 31 de diciembre de 1189, en Acre, Northern, Palestina, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Yerushalayim, Jerusalem, Israel.
